page: Microsoft Azure F1ams_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the Famsv7 server family. Powered by an AMD processor with x86_64 architecture, this instance features 1 dedicated vCPU and 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The virtual machine runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

This server configuration does not include local storage, meaning it relies on external storage volumes, though it is designated as Premium Storage capable. It does not feature any graphics processing units or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F1ams_v7 offers a specialized entry-point resource profile, providing a high memory allocation relative to its single dedicated CPU core without the overhead of local storage or GPU hardware. This makes it suitable for lightweight, memory-intensive workloads that do not require high parallel processing power, such as small-scale caching layers, lightweight databases, or simple background workers requiring a larger memory footprint.
description: Microsoft Azure F1ams_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine from the Famsv7 family, featuring a single dedicated AMD vCPU on an x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, establishing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage but is Premium Storage capable, allowing connection to external high-performance storage volumes. It contains no GPUs and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Qualitatively, the F1ams_v7 serves as a cost-efficient entry point for memory-heavy tasks that require dedicated CPU execution but do not demand multi-core processing or local disk space. This configuration is best suited for lightweight databases, small-scale caching systems, and memory-sensitive background processes.
